steps.title: Identify two findings with opposite patterns
steps.content: The passage reports two findings from the researchers' analysis: negative adjectives evolve most rapidly while negative nouns evolve most slowly—opposite extremes from the same study.

steps.title: Select the transition connecting disparate findings
steps.content:
The logical relationship is simultaneous contrast: from the same analysis, negative adjectives evolve most rapidly while negative nouns evolve most slowly — opposite extremes discovered at the same time. The blank needs a transition signaling a concurrent but distinct finding.
  • Choice A — meanwhile,: signals a concurrent but separate or contrasting fact, fitting perfectly — both findings come from the same analysis, but they go in opposite directions. ✓
  • Choice B — similarly,: signals that the second finding parallels the first, so fails — nouns evolving most slowly is the opposite pattern from adjectives evolving most rapidly. ✗
  • Choice C — hence,: signals a logical consequence, so fails — the noun finding is not caused by the adjective finding; they are independent results. ✗
  • Choice D — subsequently,: signals a later time, so fails — both findings come from the same 2022 analysis, not from different points in time. ✗
That matches Choice A — meanwhile,.
